---
title: "Visually communicate your scientific data with R: designing scientific figures"
format: 
    clean-revealjs:
        # code-fold: false
        code-overflow: wrap
        # code-block-bg: "#e60000"
        # code-block-border-left: "#31BAE9"
        code-summary: "Show code"
        highlight-style: dracula
author:
  - name: Eric Largy
    orcid: 0000-0002-6140-9788
    email: eric.largy@u-bordeaux.fr
    affiliations: 
      - ARNA, INSERM U1212, CNRS UMR 5320, Université de Bordeaux
      - UFR des Sciences Pharmaceutiques, Université de Bordeaux
date: last-modified
css: theme/custom.css
---

# Introduction to Grammar of Graphics {background-color="#427711"}

## A framework for data visualization {.smaller}

Introduced by Leland Wilkinson ([The Grammar of Graphics](https://link.springer.com/book/10.1007/0-387-28695-0 "Access provided by U. Bordeaux"), 2005).

Graphics can be built up from a set of components:

::::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
-   [Data]{.fg style="--col: #427711"}: the dataset to be visualized
-   [Variables]{.fg style="--col: #427711"}: mapping of objects to values
-   [Algebra]{.fg style="--col: #427711"}: operations to combine variables and specify dimensions
-   [Scales]{.fg style="--col: #427711"}: represent variables on measured dimensions
-   [Statistics]{.fg style="--col: #427711"}: functions to change the appearance and representation
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
-   [Geometry]{.fg style="--col: #427711"}: creation of geometric graphs from variables
-   [Coordinates]{.fg style="--col: #427711"}: mapping to coordinate systems
-   [Aesthetics]{.fg style="--col: #427711"}: sensory attributes
-   [Facets]{.fg style="--col: #427711"}: subplots based on subsets of data
-   [Guides]{.fg style="--col: #427711"}: legends and axes to explain the graph
:::
:::::

## Adapted to R in the `ggplot2` package 

![](resources/overview_graphic-1.png)

## Example: Data {.smaller}

```{r}
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| output-location: column
library(ggplot2)

head(mpg)
```
## Example: Data {auto-animate=TRUE}

```{r}
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| output-location: column
#| fig-asp: 1
#| fig-width: 5
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(mpg)
```

## Example: Mapping {auto-animate=TRUE}

```{r}
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| output-location: column
#| fig-asp: 1
#| fig-width: 5
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(
  mpg,
  mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy)
)
```

## Example: Layers {auto-animate=TRUE}
```{r}
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| output-location: column
#| fig-asp: 1
#| fig-width: 5
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(
  mpg,
  mapping = aes(
    x = cty,
    y = hwy
  )
) +
  geom_point()
```



## Example: Scales {auto-animate=TRUE}
```{r}
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| output-location: column
#| fig-asp: 1
#| fig-width: 5
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(
  mpg,
  mapping = aes(
    x = displ,
    y = hwy,
    colour = class
  )
) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_colour_viridis_d()
```

## Example: Facets {auto-animate=TRUE}
```{r}
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| output-location: column
#| fig-asp: 1
#| fig-width: 5
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(
  mpg,
  mapping = aes(
    x = displ,
    y = hwy,
    colour = class
  )
) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_colour_viridis_d() +
  facet_grid(class ~ year)
```

## Example: Coordinates {auto-animate=TRUE}
```{r}
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| output-location: column
#| fig-asp: 1
#| fig-width: 5
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(
  mpg,
  mapping = aes(
    x = displ,
    y = hwy,
    colour = class
  )
) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_colour_viridis_d() +
  facet_grid(class ~ year) +
  coord_cartesian(
    xlim = c(0, 8),
    ylim = c(10, 50)
  )
```

## Example: Theme {auto-animate=TRUE}
```{r}
#| echo: true
#| eval: true
#| output-location: column
#| fig-asp: 1
#| fig-width: 5
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(
  mpg,
  mapping = aes(
    x = displ,
    y = hwy,
    colour = class
  )
) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_colour_viridis_d() +
  facet_grid(class ~ year) +
  coord_cartesian(
    xlim = c(0, 8),
    ylim = c(10, 50)
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    axis.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
    axis.line = element_line(color = "black", size = 0.75),
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold")
  )
```
